pages.title: Typing Test — Free Online WPM & Accuracy Test
pages.desc: A free online typing test. Type the passage, watch your words-per-minute and accuracy update live, and see your score when you finish. No signup, nothing to install.
pages.blurb: A straightforward typing test: a short passage appears, you type it, and your speed (WPM) and accuracy are measured as you go. Correct letters turn dark, mistakes turn red, and the timer starts on your first keystroke.
pages.tip: Want a timed run instead? Switch the length to 1, 2, 3, or 5 minutes below, or swap the text to common words for pure speed practice.

pages.title: Typing Speed Test — Check Your WPM Free
pages.desc: Check your typing speed for free. Type the sample text and this typing speed test reports your words per minute and accuracy the moment you finish. No account needed.
pages.blurb: This typing speed test times you against a real passage and reports your words per minute the instant you finish. WPM is figured the standard way — every five correct characters counts as one word — so your score lines up with other tests.
pages.tip: For a fixed-length benchmark, pick a 1 or 3 minute run below. To practice, keep it on Full passage and go again as many times as you like.

pages.title: WPM Test — Measure Your Words Per Minute
pages.desc: A free WPM test. Type the passage and see your words-per-minute and accuracy measured live. WPM is correct characters divided by five, over minutes typed. No signup.
pages.blurb: A WPM test measures how many words per minute you can type accurately. Here WPM is correct characters over five, divided by the minutes you spent typing, which is how typing tests everywhere define a word.
pages.tip: Mistakes lower your accuracy but the passage does not auto-correct — you type it exactly as shown. Try a timed run below to compare your speed over a fixed window.

pages.title: Words Per Minute Test — Free Online WPM Check
pages.desc: Find your words per minute with this free online test. Type the sample passage and your WPM and accuracy appear live and in a final score. No download, no signup.
pages.blurb: This words per minute test shows your live speed as you type and a final score when the passage is done. The count uses the standard rule — five correct characters equal one word — so the number is comparable across sites.
pages.tip: Switch to a timed length below for a benchmark you can repeat, or change the text to common words to focus on raw finger speed.

pages.title: Typing Practice — Free Common-Word Drills Online
pages.desc: Free online typing practice. Drill the most common English words, with live WPM and accuracy so you can see yourself improve. No signup, replay as often as you like.
pages.blurb: Typing practice made simple: a stream of the most common English words to type over and over. There is no punctuation or capital letters to slow you down, just clean repetition to build speed and muscle memory.
pages.tip: Switch the text to sentences below when you want a more realistic run, or set a 1-minute timer to measure your pace before and after practicing.

pages.title: 1 Minute Typing Test — Free 60 Second WPM Test
pages.desc: A free 1 minute typing test. Type as much of the passage as you can in 60 seconds and get your words-per-minute and accuracy. Timer starts on your first keystroke.
pages.blurb: A one minute typing test: you have 60 seconds to type as much of the passage as you can, and the test scores your words per minute and accuracy when the clock runs out. The countdown starts the moment you begin typing.
pages.tip: One minute is the classic benchmark, but you can stretch it to 2, 3, or 5 minutes below for a steadier average. The passage extends itself so fast typists never run out of text.

pages.title: 2 Minute Typing Test — Free 2 Min WPM Test
pages.desc: A free 2 minute typing test. Type for two minutes and see your words-per-minute and accuracy. A longer window than one minute gives a steadier average. No signup.
pages.blurb: A two minute typing test gives a steadier read on your speed than a single minute, smoothing out a slow start or a lucky streak. Type the passage for two minutes and the test reports your words per minute and accuracy at the end.
pages.tip: Prefer a quicker check or a longer haul? Switch to 1, 3, or 5 minutes below. The text keeps extending, so you will not run out before time is up.

pages.title: 3 Minute Typing Test — Free 3 Min WPM Test
pages.desc: A free 3 minute typing test. Type for three minutes for a reliable words-per-minute average and accuracy score. The passage extends so you never run out. No signup.
pages.blurb: A three minute typing test is long enough to give a reliable average, the kind of window many typing courses use to certify speed. Type the passage for three minutes and get your words per minute and accuracy at the end.
pages.tip: Need a shorter or longer run? Pick 1, 2, or 5 minutes below. Consistency over three minutes tells you more than a fast ten-second burst.

pages.title: 5 Minute Typing Test — Free 5 Min WPM Test
pages.desc: A free 5 minute typing test. Five minutes of typing gives the steadiest words-per-minute average and tests your stamina. Accuracy tracked throughout. No signup.
pages.blurb: A five minute typing test measures both speed and stamina. Over five minutes your pace settles into a true average and small slips even out, which is why longer tests are used for job screening. Type the passage and get your score at the end.
pages.tip: Five minutes is a long haul — accuracy matters as much as speed. Drop to 1, 2, or 3 minutes below if you want a faster check.

pages.title: One Minute Typing Test — Free 60 Second WPM Test
pages.desc: A free one minute typing test. Type as much as you can in 60 seconds for your words-per-minute and accuracy score. The timer starts on your first keystroke. No signup.
pages.blurb: A one minute typing test is the quickest way to check your speed: you get 60 seconds to type the passage, and the test scores your words per minute and accuracy when time is up. The countdown begins on your first keystroke.
pages.tip: Want a steadier average? Choose 2, 3, or 5 minutes below. The passage extends itself, so there is always more text to type.

sentences: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog while the sun sets behind the hills.
A good habit takes time to build, but once it sticks it can carry you for years.
She packed her bags the night before so the morning would feel calm and unhurried.
Reading a few pages every day adds up to dozens of books over the course of a year.
The river bent around the old mill and slipped quietly under the wooden bridge.
Practice does not have to be long to be useful, but it does have to be regular.
He wrote the note by hand and left it on the table where she would find it at dawn.
The city looked different from the rooftop, smaller and stranger than at street level.
Small choices made every day tend to matter more than the big ones made once a year.
The garden needed water, so she filled the can and walked the rows before breakfast.
Learning to type well is less about speed and more about steady, even rhythm.
The train pulled in on time, and for once the platform was almost empty and still.
A cup of coffee cooled on the desk while the writer stared at the blinking cursor.
The map was old and creased, but every road on it still led somewhere worth going.
They planted the tree in spring and watched it grow taller through every season.
The best answer is often the simple one that you were too clever to notice at first.
Rain fell softly on the tin roof, and the whole house seemed to slow down with it.
He counted the stars until he lost track, then simply lay back and watched them turn.
A kind word costs nothing to give and can change the shape of an ordinary day.
The bakery opened at six, and the smell of warm bread drifted out onto the wet street.
Every long journey is really just a series of small steps taken one after another.
The old clock on the wall was ten minutes fast, and no one had ever bothered to fix it.
She learned to sail on a small lake before she ever set foot on the open sea.
Curiosity is a quiet engine that keeps running long after the excitement fades away.
The letters arrived out of order, so the story had to be pieced together backward.
Fresh snow covered the field, smooth and unbroken, until the first bird landed on it.
A well-made tool feels like an extension of the hand rather than a thing you hold.
The teacher wrote one question on the board and let the silence do the rest of the work.
They walked until the road ran out, then kept going along the narrow coastal path.
Good writing is mostly rewriting, cutting away the words that were never really needed.
